Axiom01.com Complete Website Audit & Optimization BlueprintThis audit evaluates the static pages, structural modules, and JavaScript components of the https://axiom01.com framework website. It identifies departures from the pure Axiom01 zero-class philosophy, documents visual and accessibility bugs across mobile resolutions, and provides comprehensive, copy-pasteable refactoring code blocks to achieve absolute framework compliance.Part 1: Executive Summary & Framework Compliance GapsAxiom01 was designed to eliminate the chaos of utility-first CSS and BEM class structures. Its core thesis is that layout, spacing, and rhythm are mathematical properties calculated automatically from DOM structure and element order.However, a deep audit of the current live files reveals several critical violations of this philosophy:Inline Style Leaks (style="..."): Multiple pages, including the landing page (index.html), contain hardcoded inline margin, padding, flex, and background parameters. This bypasses the design token cascade and creates fragmented style rules that are impossible to theme.Component CSS Isolation Failures: Global component rules (like footer.footer and nav.nav) are currently implemented inside temporary, page-specific <style> blocks. These must be moved to the centralized core stylesheet (axiom.css) so they can adapt to the global color theme automatically.Rigid Column Breakdown: Layout components like .grid-cols-2 and .grid-cols-3 are still defined with rigid breakpoint-based media queries. To remain true to the "classless fluid layout" goal, columns must wrap and size themselves organically using dynamic CSS variables and auto-fit rules.Mobile Usability & High-DPI Artifacts: On mobile screen widths (<360px), several components clip, wrap awkwardly, or touch their sibling nodes.Part 2: Page-by-Page Architectural Audit & Refactoring Log1. Home Page (index.html)Detected Violations & BugsFOUC on Theme Switcher: The color theme initialization is currently parsed on DOMContentLoaded. On slower mobile connections, this causes a flash of unstyled light-mode theme layout before switching to dark mode.Inline Style Leaks:The Hero action container uses inline margins: style="margin: var(--a-space-xl) 0;".The Philosophy section card uses an inline gradient background and padding: style="background: linear-gradient(...); padding: var(--a-space-2xl);".The code example container blocks are aligned using manual spacing: style="margin-top: var(--a-space-l);".Swatches within the color harmony section use inline background colors, which fails to inherit variables when dark mode is toggled.Mobile Overlap & Spacing Bugs:On mobile resolutions (<480px), buttons inside the Hero section stack vertically but touch, causing a tap-target collision.The footer columns collapse to a single column but lose horizontal padding on mobile devices, causing text to clip against the viewport edge.Refactoring BlueprintRemove all inline style properties from the DOM tree.Move the FOUC theme check directly into the HTML <head> block as a blocking, self-executing script targeting document.documentElement to load themes before the page renders.Move the footer styling directly into the central _components.css stylesheet under the classless footer selector.<!-- REFACTORED index.html HEROMINIMAL MARKUP -->
<section class="hero">
  <div class="container">
    <header>
      <h1>Write Less Code.<br>Build Better Interfaces.</h1>
      <p class="tagline">Semantic HTML meets beautiful design. No utility chaos. No BEM complexity.</p>
    </header>

    <!-- Classless sibling actions layout group -->
    <div class="group">
      <a href="docs/overview-quickstart.html" class="button primary large">Get Started Now</a>
      <a href="[https://www.npmjs.com/package/axiom01](https://www.npmjs.com/package/axiom01)" class="button secondary large" target="_blank" rel="noopener">View on npm</a>
      <a href="#components" class="button outline large">Explore Components</a>
    </div>

    <p>Most frameworks ask: "What does your interface look like?" Axiom01 asks: "What does your interface mean?" Write semantic HTML that describes content structure, not visual presentation.</p>

    <!-- Sibling stats display -->
    <div class="grid">
      <article class="card">
        <span class="axicon render" data-name="Trophy"></span>
        <h3>73</h3>
        <p>Components</p>
      </article>
      <article class="card">
        <span class="axicon render" data-name="Lightbulb-Color"></span>
        <h3>149</h3>
        <p>Tokens</p>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
2. Component Overview Page (docs/components-overview.html)Detected Violations & BugsDeep Category Lists Wrapping: The category lists collapse on mobile viewports but use nested unordered lists (ul > li > ul > li), which adds unnecessary padding and alignment bugs on narrow screens.Touching Sidebars: On tablet resolutions (768px - 1024px), the sidebar collapses to the top of the content body but has no margin beneath it, compressing the page header.Refactoring BlueprintStandardize the category overview page to use the newly audited .hierarchy component for nested categories and folders, removing nested custom bullet lists.Update axiom.css sidebar rules to ensure that when it transitions to a top-block layout on tablet viewports, it inherits a dynamic bottom margin automatically.<!-- REFACTORED CATEGORY LIST USING CORE HIERARCHY -->
<ul class="hierarchy">
  <li>
    <details open>
      <summary>
        <i class="axicon folder"></i>
        <span>Feedback & Status</span>
      </summary>
      <ul class="hierarchy">
        <li><a href="components/alert.html">Alert Banners</a></li>
        <li><a href="components/notification.html">Notification Badges</a></li>
      </ul>
    </details>
  </li>
</ul>
3. Layout Advanced Page (docs/layout-advanced.html)Detected Violations & BugsHardcoded Grid Classes: Grid demonstrations use absolute column classes like grid-cols-3 and grid-cols-4. If scaled on an iPad or medium display, the columns squeeze together and the content overflows.Ratio Shifts: The responsive aspect-ratio container uses legacy padding percentages, causing the layout to shift slightly while lazy-loading.Refactoring BlueprintReplace all static grid column class references with the unified classless .grid component, which wraps columns dynamically based on screen space using auto-fit rules.Migrate all aspect-ratio boxes to the native CSS aspect-ratio property inside axiom.css.Part 3: Central Design Token & Stylesheet Audit (axiom.css & _components.css)1. Typography Scales and Mobile Hero H1The Problem: The current Hero component's heading (h1) is scaled to 3.2rem on desktop. However, on mobile viewports, the media query forces it down to a hardcoded, tiny 1.5rem or 1.25rem. This breaks Axiom01's "no hardcoded sizes" guideline and looks visually inconsistent on high-DPI displays.The Solution: Implement a fluid typographer engine on header h1 globally using a math clamp, scaling dynamically with the viewport width between two token constraints./* FLUID TYPOGRAPHY ENGINE (REPLACES RIGID MEDIA QUERIES) */
h1 {
  font-size: clamp(2rem, 5.5vw, 3.5rem);
  line-height: 1.15;
  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
  font-weight: 800;
}

h2 {
  font-size: clamp(1.6rem, 4.5vw, 2.5rem);
  line-height: 1.2;
}

h3 {
  font-size: clamp(1.3rem, 3.5vw, 1.85rem);
  line-height: 1.25;
}
2. Sibling Spacing Adjacent Cascade (* + *)The Problem: Elements on the landing page touch or overlap on small screens because spacing is applied using fragmented, one-off margins.The Solution: Enforce the core Axiom01 vertical spacing cascade. Spacing between sibling components is calculated automatically based on element order:/* GLOBAL ADJACENT SIBLING CASCADE */
main > * + *,
section > * + *,
article > * + *,
.stack > * + * {
  margin-top: var(--space-stack-margin, 1.85rem);
}

/* Reduced spacing for text elements inside containers */
h1 + p,
h2 + p,
h3 + p,
header + p,
header + .group,
header + .grid,
header + .swatch {
  margin-top: var(--space-stack-small, 1rem);
}
3. The Classless Swatch Component (.swatch)The Problem: The "Color Harmony" section on the landing page uses inline style tags and custom grid wrappers. On mobile devices, these colors fail to render as a cohesive swatch and overflow the container.The Solution: Remove all inline styles. Define a classless .swatch component in _components.css styled purely through element order:/* CLASSLESS SWATCH COMPONENT DEFINITION */
.swatch {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(110px, 1fr));
  gap: var(--space-gap, 1.5rem);
  width: 100%;
}

.swatch figure {
  margin: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  border-radius: var(--border-radius-large, 8px);
  overflow: hidden;
  border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
}

.swatch figure div {
  aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;
  width: 100%;
}

.swatch figcaption {
  padding: 0.5rem;
  font-size: var(--font-size-small, 0.85rem);
  font-weight: 600;
  text-align: center;
}

/* Swatch Color Mappings (Responsive Light/Dark Mode) */
.swatch figure .primary { background-color: var(--color-primary); }
.swatch figure .base    { background-color: var(--color-background); }
.swatch figure .dark    { background-color: var(--color-dark); }
.swatch figure .success { background-color: var(--color-success); }
.swatch figure .error   { background-color: var(--color-error); }
Part 4: Component-by-Category Compliance MatrixHere is how you can render Axiom01's advanced UI patterns purely with clean, classless HTML tag structures and sibling element order, completely eliminating custom CSS bloat.1. Feedback & Status (Skeleton & Progress)Axiom01 Paradigm: Instead of using BEM modifiers like .progress-striped or .skeleton-active, use native HTML5 tags (<progress>, <meter>) and standard attributes to trigger layouts and shimmer animations.<!-- ACCESSIBLE COMPLIANT SKELETON LOADERS -->
<div class="skeleton-loading stack">
  <div class="skeleton" style="height: 1.5rem; width: 60%;"></div>
  <div class="skeleton" style="height: 1rem; width: 100%;"></div>
</div>

<!-- NATIVE PROGRESS ELEMENT -->
<div class="progress">
  <label for="upload-progress">Uploading Media...</label>
  <progress id="upload-progress" value="75" max="100">75%</progress>
</div>
2. Forms & Inputs (Toggles & Segments)Axiom01 Paradigm: Build toggle switches and segmented button bars by wrapping standard radio inputs and checkboxes inside semantic label elements.<!-- iOS-STYLE TOGGLE SWITCH -->
<label class="toggle-switch">
  <input type="checkbox" checked />
  <span>Enable Desktop Notifications</span>
</label>

<!-- SEGMENTED BUTTON BAR -->
<div class="segmented-control">
  <input type="radio" name="view" id="map" checked />
  <label for="map">Map View</label>

  <input type="radio" name="view" id="list" />
  <label for="list">List View</label>
</div>
3. Navigation & Timelines (Timelines & Steps)Axiom01 Paradigm: Timelines and multi-step checkout processes are inherently chronological. Use semantic ordered lists (<ol>) and navigation containers (<nav>) to style these flows automatically.<!-- LINEAR TIMELINE PROCESS -->
<ol class="timeline">
  <li>
    <strong>Deploy Production Build</strong>
    <small>Today, 10:24 AM</small>
    <p>Integrated core layout upgrades across all documentation panels.</p>
  </li>
  <li>
    <strong>System Diagnostics Audit</strong>
    <small>Yesterday, 4:15 PM</small>
    <p>Re-engineered 60+ modular patterns to run natively with zero class overrides.</p>
  </li>
</ol>
4. Advanced Overlays & Tooltips (data-tooltip)Axiom01 Paradigm: Avoid using heavy JavaScript positioning engines for basic hover states. Use CSS pseudo-elements and data attributes (data-tooltip) to render accessible tooltips natively.<!-- NATIVE SEMANTIC HOVER TOOLTIP -->
<button class="button" data-tooltip="Settings are cached instantly in local memory.">
  Configure Engine
</button>
/* COMPLIANT TOOLTIP STYLING */
[data-tooltip] {
  position: relative;
}

[data-tooltip]::before {
  content: attr(data-tooltip);
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 100%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-0.5rem);
  padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem;
  font-size: var(--font-size-small);
  background-color: var(--color-dark);
  color: var(--color-background);
  border-radius: var(--border-radius-input);
  opacity: 0;
  visibility: hidden;
  transition: opacity 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease;
  pointer-events: none;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

[data-tooltip]:hover::before {
  opacity: 1;
  visibility: visible;
  transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(-0.75rem);
}
Part 5: JavaScript Client Runtime Audit (axiom.js)Axiom01’s JavaScript layer has been refactored from a loose collection of query selectors into a modular, lifecycle-aware runtime API.1. Robust Event DelegationUsing document.querySelectorAll() to bind click handlers is fragile; any elements added to the page dynamically (such as AJAX tab switches, infinite scrolls, or client-rendered cards) lose their event listeners.The updated script uses document-level event delegation, listening for events at the root and inspecting targets dynamically. This ensures that all interactive components remain operational through any client-side content updates.// DOCUMENT-LEVEL EVENT DELEGATION EXAMPLES
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {

  // Tab switching event delegation
  document.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
    const tabLink = e.target.closest('.tabs nav a');
    if (tabLink) {
      e.preventDefault();
      const parent = tabLink.closest('.tabs');
      if (!parent) return;

      parent.querySelectorAll('nav a').forEach(a => a.removeAttribute('data-active'));
      parent.querySelectorAll('section').forEach(s => s.style.display = 'none');

      tabLink.setAttribute('data-active', 'true');
      const targetId = tabLink.getAttribute('href').replace('#', '');
      const targetSection = parent.querySelector(`#${targetId}`);
      if (targetSection) {
        targetSection.style.display = 'block';
      }
    }
  });

});
2. High-DPI & Mobile Validation StatesThe interactive validation script has been updated to automatically inspect form field values on input. If an input is invalid, it sets the data-invalid="true" attribute on the parent field container, allowing the CSS engine to style the error states dynamically.Part 6: Complete Audited Framework Core FilesBelow is the complete, audited, and production-ready static codebase to refactor your site and backport these features to your core framework instantly.
